requirement.title: Realtime mic audio capture
requirement.goal: Capture microphone input continuously as PCM frames suitable for Deepgram streaming. (source: test step 1 + Python audio constraint)

provenance.source: Test brief step 1: User speaks → realtime transcription via Deepgram. Python mandated. sounddevice chosen per CONDUIT-PROJ-D-005.

acceptance.criterion: sounddevice InputStream opens and delivers PCM buffers to Deepgram websocket without underrun.

requirement.title: Realtime Deepgram transcription with <300ms word-to-saved latency
requirement.goal: Stream mic audio to Deepgram's websocket API, receive word-level timestamps, write each word's chars to the unified data store within 300ms of utterance. (source: test step 1)

provenance.source: Test brief step 1: latency target <300ms from word-spoken to word-saved. Deepgram websocket + interim_results=True per CONDUIT-PROJ-D-002.

acceptance.criterion: Words appear in data store within 300ms of being spoken (measured from Deepgram is_final callback timestamp).
Deepgram websocket uses interim_results=True and punctuate=True.

requirement.title: Unified char-level data format (char, start_time, end_time, notes)
requirement.goal: Every char spoken by user OR heard from TTS lands in one append-only store with consistent schema: char, start_time, end_time, notes. (source: test steps 2 + 6)

provenance.source: Test brief steps 2+6: data format char, start-time, end-time, notes/debug. One entry per char. "hello world" = 11 or 12 entries. Applies to both user speech and TTS output.

acceptance.criterion: "hello world" produces exactly 11 entries (space is a char, count as needed per test spec — 12 if space included).
Each entry has char, start_time (float seconds), end_time (float seconds), notes (string).
User speech and bot TTS chars coexist in single JSONL file with source tag in notes.

requirement.title: Char-level timestamp derivation via linear interpolation
requirement.goal: When API gives word-level times, interpolate evenly across chars (linear by char index). Document the method in notes column. (source: test step 3)

provenance.source: Test brief step 3: Word start/end times from Deepgram; char-level timestamps estimated by interpolation across word duration.

acceptance.criterion: char_start = word_start + (char_idx / char_count) * word_duration for each char in word.
notes field says "interpolated" or "native" to distinguish derived from API-provided timestamps.

requirement.title: LLM response generation via Groq
requirement.goal: Send transcribed user turn to LLM (Groq recommended for latency), receive text response ready for TTS. (source: test step 4)

provenance.source: Test brief step 4: Transcription to LLM response. Operator choice: Groq/Fireworks/Cerebras/OpenRouter. Groq chosen per CONDUIT-PROJ-D-003.

acceptance.criterion: Full user turn text sent to Groq API; non-empty response received.
LLM call completes in <2s for typical conversational turns.

requirement.title: ElevenLabs TTS with human voice and char-level timestamps
requirement.goal: Convert LLM response to audio + char-level timestamps; play audio for user; log bot chars in unified store. (source: test step 5)

provenance.source: Test brief steps 5+6: LLM response to human-sounding TTS via ElevenLabs. Char-level timestamps of TTS output saved in same format. Per CONDUIT-PROJ-D-004.

acceptance.criterion: ElevenLabs text_to_speech.with_timestamps returns char-level timestamps; these are stored in unified format.
Audio plays to user via sounddevice with no noticeable cut-off.

requirement.title: End-to-end conversation loop
requirement.goal: Continuous conversation where user speaks, bot responds, user speaks again; all chars (spoken + heard) logged in single unified store across all turns. (source: test final-product paragraph)

provenance.source: Test brief end state: program where user converses with LLM (human voice), all spoken+heard chars saved in unified format.

acceptance.criterion: Program runs indefinitely until Ctrl+C; each turn appends to the same JSONL file.
Bot chars have notes="bot,native" or "bot,interpolated"; user chars have notes="user,interpolated".

requirement.title: Crosstalk for intelligent interruptions [Part 2]
requirement.goal: Implement tarzain/crosstalk logic so LLM begins generating before user finishes; cancel if user continues speaking; minimize cut-offs. (source: test Part 2)

provenance.source: Test brief Part 2: implement crosstalk per https://github.com/tarzain/crosstalk. Familiarize with repo logic + impl. Integrate into Part 1. Per CONDUIT-PROJ-D-007.

acceptance.criterion: LLM generation begins before user finishes speaking (speculative start).
If user continues speaking after speculative start threshold, generation is cancelled cleanly.
Existing char data format unchanged by crosstalk integration.
